Name: Y&H MPPT
Note: One of the more popular MPPT units on the market. I still think it will be of limited use here, because the vast majority of MPPT units only contain a buck converter that lowers the voltage to match the battery. They cannot raise the voltage, because they are mainly used with PV systems that already operate at high voltage, while our generator is low-voltage because of the high kV. Even after rewinding the motor, the voltage will still likely be too low, and an MPPT with voltage step-up capability is basically not available at a reasonable price due to poor availability.

Name: artificial sun - lamp for incubating eggs / terrariums, because the goal is a lot of infrared
Note: The only readily available "artificial sun" option I could find among infrared lamps.

Name: spray mounting adhesive
Note: Will be used to attach the Mylar film to the dish.

Name: 21700 Li-Ion cells
Note: With the option of preheating the nickel tabs so the soldering is more reliable. One extra as a spare.

Name: BLDC motor 6374 170kV
Note: Good power reserve and no need for rewinding, but I am not sure whether the price is not simply too high, so I am leaving a cheaper alternative below.

Name: BLDC brushless motor N3536 1000kV 2-4S
Note: Cheaper, but because the kV is too high it would need to be rewound, which could still be interesting.

Name: 0.3 mm magnet wire
Note: A good reserve for repeat attempts if the winding does not work out.

Name: Schottky diode 60V 5A
Note: Will be used to rectify the voltage from the generator.

Name: electrolytic capacitor 1 mF 50V low-ESR
Note: To smooth the voltages after the rectifier,

Name: ceramic capacitor 2.2 uF 50V
Note: To suppress fast voltage spikes after the rectifier,

Name: NPN transistor BD139
Note: Will serve as an emitter follower.

Name: 12V 1W Zener diode
Note: Will be used in the emitter follower to limit the voltage supplied to the MOSFET gate drivers to around 12V.

Name: wirewound inductor 10 uH
Note: Will be used in the boost converter.

Name: MOSFET gate driver MCP1407-E/P
Note: Should work well and efficiently for MOSFET switching in the boost converter,

Name: N-MOSFET transistor WMK030N06HG4WAYON
Note: Acts as the switch for the boost converter.

Name: LM7805 voltage regulator
Note: Voltage regulator for the Arduino. I considered using another emitter follower, but the voltage there would be too unstable,

Name: ACS712 20A current sensor
Note: Required for the calculations used by the P&O algorithm.

Name: 33 kΩ resistor ±0.1%
Note: Both resistors will be used to create a voltage divider acting as a voltage measurement circuit, also required for P&O.

Name: black heat-resistant paint
Note: Will be used to coat the cylinder heated by solar radiation.

Name: aluminum rod 60x50 mm
Note: Will be used to make the pistons.

Name: stainless steel pipe 60 mm inner diameter
Note: Will be used to make the cylinder.

Name: needle bearing 10 mm inner diameter
Note: Will be used for bearing support.

Name: stainless steel rod fi10
Note: Will be used for the pistons, the connecting rod, and the connection at the flywheel.

Name: 2 mm stainless steel sheet 20x20 cm
Note: Will be used to cap off the cylinders.

Name: 0.5 mm copper sheet 10x10 cm
Note: Will be used to make cooling fins.

Name: reinforced silicone hose 1 m 20x28
Note: Will be used to connect the cold cylinder to the hot one.

Name: self-aligning bearing 10 mm
Note: Will be used to mount the flywheel.

Name: cylinder hone 32-89 mm
Note: Necessary to polish the cylinder evenly with a crosshatch finish.
